contract_claim: LONG literal parsing strips the L/l suffix then calls Long.parseLong(text). If the original literal used a hex prefix (0xDEADBEEFL), the suffix is stripped but the '0x' prefix remains, so Long.parseLong('0xDEADBEEF') will throw NumberFormatException. The INTEGER branch at L2080 handles 0x/0b, but the LONG branch does not.
observed_behavior: LONG literal parsing strips the L/l suffix then calls Long.parseLong(text). If the original literal used a hex prefix (0xDEADBEEFL), the suffix is stripped but the '0x' prefix remains, so Long.parseLong('0xDEADBEEF') will throw NumberFormatException. The INTEGER branch at L2080 handles 0x/0b, but the LONG branch does not.
